name: Rahima Foundation
mission: Today, the world celebrates vast technological advancements, yet every 9th person suffers from food scarcity (1). The rate of food insecurity in the United States is even higher, standing at 11.8% (2). In the Silicon Valley, specifically, that rate more than doubles to 24.8%—making every 4th person food insecure (3). A total of 720,000 people out of 2.9 million Bay Area residents live with food insecurity (3).
theoryOfChange: Rahima Foundation states it provides a safety net to low-income community members through monthly groceries, rent and utility assistance, hot meals, and educational support, with a mission of creating equitable, resilient and inclusive communities starting with access to nutritious food for all.
scoreSummary: RAHIMA INTERNATIONAL FOUNDATION shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Rahima Foundation explicitly lists "Zakat" under its programs and services. For example, their website states, "WHAT ELSE ARE WE DOING? ... Zakat." and "OUR PROGRAMS ... Zakat." Another source confirms, "The programs that Rahima Foundation offers: food distribution, soup kitchen, meat collection, zakat and sadaqa collection, student needs." Additionally, a charity detail page for Rahima International Foundation (a 501(c)3 Charitable & Educational Foundation based in the US) lists it as "Zakat eligible." (Source: https://www.rahima.org/general-donations-rahima)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 65 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: Claims 9 geographic areas but no foreign grants on Schedule F
keyConcerns.detail: Organization lists international operations but IRS filings show no foreign grant activity.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 29/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, domestic poverty cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: The impact rationale mentions a cost per beneficiary of $1616.14, but doesn't explicitly connect this to the 'average' impact tier. It also doesn't mention the weak board governance (2 members) which significantly impacted the score.

The amal_score_rationale mentions a 'medium impact directness and donor alignment profile,' but the alignment is actually medium and the impact is also medium. It could be clearer to state that both dimensions are average.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows a medium donor fit for those prioritizing domestic poverty, specifically within the context of zakat-eligible services [2]. It maintains a strong track record of community engagement through structured volunteer opportunities and diverse donation channels [4][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity demonstrates high financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 96.0/100 and a 95.3% program expense ratio [1]. While it provides direct services like meat collection and food distribution, the calculated cost per beneficiary is approximately $1,616.14, reflecting a medium impact score [3].

baseline_narrative.summary: Based in California, Rahima International Foundation focuses on alleviating domestic poverty through food distribution, soup kitchens, and educational assistance [1][3]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various giving methods [2][4].

credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should expand its <cite id="16">2-member board</cite> to meet standard governance benchmarks of at least 5 independent members.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and want their zakat to support local Muslim-led poverty alleviation in Northern California.

Prefer supporting established local institutions with long histories
Seek to fulfill zakat through a charity with a Gold transparency seal
giving_considerations: The charity has a very high reserve-to-expense ratio
Governance is concentrated in a very small board
Revenue has seen a slight decline with a -5.2% CAGR
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-growth organizations or those who require a large, diverse board of directors for governance comfort.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: While the charity has a documented theory of change and high transparency, it lacks independent third-party impact evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The charity uses direct food and financial assistance to stabilize households, enabling students to focus on education.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the high program spending effectively reduces long-term poverty for beneficiaries.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the charity has high reserves, its declining revenue trend suggests a need for renewed donor support to maintain its scale.

strengths.detail: The foundation maintains a <cite id="5">95.3% program expense ratio</cite> and spends <cite id="6">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
The organization holds an <cite id="8">overall score of 96.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="9">Gold Seal of Transparency</cite> from Candid.
Operating since <cite id="10">1993</cite>, the charity has a long track record of serving <cite id="11">San Mateo, Alameda, and Santa Clara counties</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Rahima International Foundation exhibits a rare financial profile where <cite id="5">95.3% of expenses are dedicated to programs</cite>. This is supported by a <cite id="12">95.3% independent board</cite> and a <cite id="13">perfect score of 100 for Culture & Community</cite> from Charity Navigator. Their ability to maintain <cite id="7">60.7 months of operating reserves</cite> provides an incredible safety net, ensuring that their <cite id="14">food and educational assistance programs</cite> can continue even during economic downturns. Furthermore, the charity manages <cite id="15">$14,151,875 in total assets</cite> with virtually no liabilities, reflecting a very conservative and stable financial management style.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1993, Rahima International Foundation is a California-based humanitarian organization dedicated to <cite id="1">feeding the hungry and assisting the financially insecure</cite>. The charity operates primarily in the San Francisco Bay Area, providing <cite id="2">food distribution, soup kitchens, and meat collection</cite> services to vulnerable populations. With over three decades of experience, the foundation has established itself as a <cite id="3">zakat-eligible institution</cite> that serves multiple categories of asnaf, including the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin). Financially, the organization operates with extreme leaness, reporting <cite id="4">$4,006,022 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. A defining characteristic of their model is their <cite id="5">95.3% program expense ratio</cite>, meaning every dollar of functional expense is directed toward program services rather than administrative overhead. This efficiency is further highlighted by a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that the foundation does not spend donor funds on professional solicitation. While the charity maintains <cite id="7">strong liquidity with 65.0 months of working capital</cite>, this high level of reserves suggests a significant opportunity to expand their direct service delivery to meet growing local needs.
